caption.text:
🌳🌎🌳 Trees are far more than the towering shapes we glimpse against the sky; they are dynamic networks of life, deeply entangled with the world around them. 

To truly see a tree is to recognise its complexity—a vibrant system where water rises from root to crown, where sugars flow downward from leaf to soil, nourishing an underground web of mycelium that stretches like a neural network beneath our feet.

Of the Oak is an invitation to witness the tree as a living monument of connection, a keystone in the web of life.

3rd May - 28th September 2025 
Royal Botanic Gardens Kew London

Commissioned by the RBG Kew.

#OfTheOak #MLF #MarshmallowLaserFeast #KewGardens

caption.text:
Through the lens of satellite data we have been exploring photosynthesis on a global scale. 

'Every breath we take is part of the global carbon cycle, connecting us directly to the planet’s breathing system.' – Miguel Mahecha

When experiencing You:Matter at the Science and Media Museum in Bradford, you’ll step into a room that pulses with the breath of the planet itself: a vast circular screen, alive with satellite data, visualising the Earth’s day/night cycle of carbon dioxide — pulsing in a rhythmic fashion, reminiscent of a breathing, beating Earth.

The science of the carbon cycle, a fragile balance made visible, and visceral.

You:Matter, an exhibition commissioned for 
Bradford UK City of Culture 2025
Until 28 February 2026.

Many thanks to @arteo.earth for their detailed advice on EO datasets, models, and background info.

#YouMatterExhibition #BreathingPlanet #CarbonCycle #MarshmallowLaserFeast

From the stars above, to each living cell—from the air we share to the water and light coursing through our bodies— YOU:MATTER makes the invisible visible. 

🎟️ Tickets available from £2 via the National Science and Media Museum website.

caption.text:
🌊 On World Oceans Day, we’re reflecting on the remarkable journey of 𝘚𝘦𝘦𝘪𝘯𝘨 𝘌𝘤𝘩𝘰𝘦𝘴 𝘪𝘯 𝘵𝘩𝘦 𝘔𝘪𝘯𝘥 𝘰𝘧 𝘵𝘩𝘦 𝘞𝘩𝘢𝘭𝘦.

What began as an attempt to imagine the world through cetacean senses is now resonating across four continents. Today marks the closing of the work’s presentation at the Venice Biennale, while new encounters continue to unfold around the globe, include at Artscience museum Singapore where it just opened for new audiences.

Fittingly, every home this work has found sits beside a body of water. From Barcelona’s Mediterranean shoreline, to Montréal where the St. Lawrence meets the Atlantic, and Singapore’s harbour edge. In Venice, inside a former 15th-century convent on the island of Giudecca, where the New York Times described visitors finding themselves figuratively plunging underwater, the Venetian lagoon itself part of the soundscape.

Created in collaboration with filmmaker Tom Mustill, the work is an invitation to slip beyond the limits of our own perception and into the sensory worlds of whales. It emerged through the generosity of bioacousticians, marine biologists, researchers, filmmakers, and ocean advocates who helped us glimpse how these extraordinary beings might experience the world.

At a time when the oceans face unprecedented pressures, we remain grateful for every opportunity to create encounters that inspire wonder, and kinship with beings we are just beginning to understand.

📍 As Above, So Below, Venice Biennale collateral event (closed 8 June) @zeitgeist19 
📍 WA Museum, Perth — until 12 June 2026
📍 @artsciencemuseumsg Singapore — until 1 November 2026
📍 Panorama Expérience, Montréal — until 27 October 2026
More tides ahead. 🌊

𝘚𝘦𝘦𝘪𝘯𝘨 𝘌𝘤𝘩𝘰𝘦𝘴 𝘪𝘯 𝘵𝘩𝘦 𝘔𝘪𝘯𝘥 𝘰𝘧 𝘵𝘩𝘦 𝘞𝘩𝘢𝘭𝘦 was commissioned by Fundación Telefónica and DHub Barcelona, and first presented as part of Echoes of the Ocean, curated by @jldevicente and Maria Brancós Barti.

caption.text:
Seeing Echoes in the Mind of the Whale is open to public today! We are thrilled to be part of Into the Ocean: Journey Beneath at the ArtScience Museum of Singapore. @artsciencemuseumsg 

We are grateful for Honor Harger’s @honorharger guiding light and invitation, as well as ASM museum’s dedicated team’s huge efforts to create a wonderful show. 

Seeing Echoes In the Mind of the Whale was created in collaboration with Tom Mustill @tommustill and with the support of the UPC Bioacoustic Applications Laboratory. Conceived in dialogue with Jose Luis De Vincente @jldevicente of Fast.

[From the exhibition text]
Into the Ocean: Journey Beneath is a collaboration between ArtScience Museum and @oceanx OceanX, making its world premiere in Singapore. It invites visitors on a descent through the depths of the sea, from the sunlit surface waters to the darkest reaches of the ocean.

As visitors descend through the ocean’s zones, from the visible, life‑rich surface to the deepest reaches of the sea, OceanX’s footage is placed in dialogue with contemporary artworks and scientific specimens. The exhibition features works by internationally renowned artists such as Marshmallow Laser Feast, Marco Barotti, Jana Winderen, Robertina Šebjanič, Lachlan Turczan, Sissel Tolaas, Ong Kian Peng and bit.studio, alongside regional scientific research such as National University of Singapore’s long-term coral reef studies. Together, these perspectives illuminate the ocean as a dynamic, interconnected system that sustains life on Earth.

6 Jun – 1 Nov 2026
ArtScience Museum
Marina Bay Sands, Singapore

caption.text:
We welcome @marshmallowlaserfeast as our latest ArtScientists-in-Residence, marking a new chapter in our ongoing exploration of the hidden systems that shape the natural world.
 
Their practice translates abstract systems into embodied, sensory experiences, inviting deeper reflection on the unseen ecosystems.
 
The collective will present a trilogy of major works, marking their most extensive engagement in Asia.

Join us for the residency-focused symposium, where Director @ersinhanersin will share deeper insights into their work.
 
Symposium tickets are available via our link in bio.

-
 
Evolver, an Atlas V, Pressman Film, Dirty Films, Marshmallow Laser Feast and Orange production. 

Executive produced by Edward R. Pressman and Terrence Malick, featuring voice over by Cate Blanchett and music by Jonny Greenwood, Meredith Monk, Jóhann Jóhannsson, Howard Skempton and Jon Hopkins.

 📷: Marshmallow Laser Feast, Evolver, 2022, Courtesy of the artist.

-

Seeing Echoes in the Mind of the Whale, an artwork by Marshmallow Laser Feast in collaboration with Tom Mustill and with the scientific support of the UPC Bioacoustic Applications Laboratory. 

Executive produced by Carolina Vallejo, Eleanor (Nell) Whitley, Mike Jones and sound and spatialisation by Simon Hendry.

📷: Marshmallow Laser Feast, Seeing Echoes in the Mind of the Whale, 2024, Courtesy of the artist.

-
 
Poetics of Soil: Fly Agaric I, 2025, an artwork by Marshmallow Laser Feast. 

Executive produced by Carolina Vallejo, Eleanor (Nell) Whitley, Mike Jones and music, sound design and spatialisation by James Bulley.

📷: Marshmallow Laser Feast, Poetics of Soil: Fly Agaric I, 2025, Courtesy of the artist.

caption.text:
AS ABOVE, SO BELOW 
Collateral Event of @labiennale 
Now on view until June 8 ✨ 

Presenting Seeing Echoes in the Mind of the Whale (2024–2025) by Marshmallow Laser Feast — an immersive audiovisual installation exploring the sensory world of cetaceans through sound, perception, and deep listening. 

Following the journeys of a bottlenose dolphin, a humpback whale, and a sperm whale, the work invites audiences to encounter the ocean through the senses of these extraordinary beings — navigating through vibration, resonance, memory, and song. 

Developed in collaboration with filmmaker Tom Mustill and the UPC Bioacoustic Applications Laboratory, the installation bridges scientific research and artistic expression, creating a profound meditation on our relationship with the living ocean. 

Organised by @oneoceanfound
Ideated and conceived by @ZEITGEIST19

Curated by 
@ElizabethZhivkova & @FarahPiriyeCoene

Monday - Friday | 10:00 - 18:00
Factory H3. Campo Cosmo, Giudecca, Venice
@serendpt_venezia 
Free entry

Credit: Marshmallow Laser Feast, Seeing Echoes in the Mind of the Whale, Venice, 2026. Courtesy of the artist, photo Ersin Han Ersin

•

Presentiamo Seeing Echoes in the Mind of the Whale (2024–2025) di Marshmallow Laser Feast — un’installazione audiovisiva immersiva che esplora il mondo sensoriale dei cetacei attraverso il suono, la percezione e l’ascolto profondo. 

Seguendo i viaggi di un delfino tursiope, una megattera e un capodoglio, l’opera invita il pubblico a vivere l’oceano attraverso i sensi di questi esseri straordinari — navigando tra vibrazione, risonanza, memoria e canto. 

#BiennaleArte2026 #InMinorKeys
#AsAboveSoBelow

caption.text:
𝘚𝘦𝘦𝘪𝘯𝘨 𝘌𝘤𝘩𝘰𝘦𝘴 𝘪𝘯 𝘵𝘩𝘦 𝘔𝘪𝘯𝘥 𝘰𝘧 𝘵𝘩𝘦 𝘞𝘩𝘢𝘭𝘦 returns as part of 𝘈𝘴 𝘈𝘣𝘰𝘷𝘦, 𝘚𝘰 𝘉𝘦𝘭𝘰𝘸, opening 9 May as a Collateral Event of the 61st International Art Exhibition – La Biennale di Venezia.

Curated by Elizabeth Zhivkova and Farah Piriye Coene of ZEITGEIST19, the exhibition is set inside the former Church of Saints Cosmas and Damian on Giudecca, the exhibition traces the connections between cosmos and Earth, atmosphere and abyss, human and more-than-human. In this context, the work takes audiences beneath the surface and into the sensory world of cetaceans, a bridge between our imagination and their umwelt, their lived sensory perspective.

Presented alongside Antoine Bertin @ant1bert1 , Yoko Shimizu, Almagul Menlibayeva, Andrea Crespi, Orkhan Mammadov, and Elnara Nasirli @elnara.nasirli

caption.text:
Happy Earth Day 🌍

Day turns to night, night turns to day, an exhale and an inhale. Wherever you are today, try this: breathe in as the sun rises over the Earth, and breathe out as it falls into night. Feel your rhythm slow. This is resonant breathing, a technique that steadies your system, fostering calm and focus.

The Earth rotates slowly before you. You float. From this perspective, the atmosphere is the co-creation of all breathing beings, an invisible current streaming through forests, across oceans, between mouths and leaves.

Astronauts call this the Overview Effect, that moment, seen from space, when borders dissolve and the planet is felt as one living, breathing whole. To breathe is to participate, to enter the stream of life’s constant exchange.

Artwork:
𝘛𝘩𝘦 𝘉𝘳𝘦𝘢𝘵𝘩𝘪𝘯𝘨 𝘗𝘭𝘢𝘯𝘦𝘵 — 𝘞𝘪𝘯𝘥 𝘋𝘢𝘵𝘢 (𝘑𝘢𝘯–𝘍𝘦𝘣 𝟤𝟢𝟤𝟢)
Architecture by @atelier522 
Soundscape by Carolyn Downing & @kate.fleur.young 
Commissioned by Thermengruppe Josef Wund @badeweltsinsheim

caption.text:
🪐𝘋𝘪𝘴𝘵𝘰𝘳𝘵𝘪𝘰𝘯𝘴 𝘪𝘯 𝘚𝘱𝘢𝘤𝘦𝘵𝘪𝘮𝘦 is one of the artworks presented in 𝘉𝘳𝘦𝘢𝘵𝘩𝘦 | 𝘔𝘢𝘶𝘳𝘪 𝘖𝘳𝘢: 𝘈𝘯 𝘐𝘮𝘮𝘦𝘳𝘴𝘪𝘷𝘦 𝘑𝘰𝘶𝘳𝘯𝘦𝘺 𝘪𝘯𝘵𝘰 𝘕𝘢𝘵𝘶𝘳𝘦, our monographic touring exhibition that traces our interconnectedness to the more-than-human world.

In creating the work, we listened into black holes, encountering the insight that the Big Bang produced only the lightest elements — hydrogen and helium — while all others were forged in the thermonuclear cores of dying stars. As Carl Sagan put it:
“Our existence hinges on these dying stars; the atoms composing everything we hold dear were once part of a star. Recognising that life’s source springs from them greatly deepens our sense of wonder for the interconnectedness of the living world.”

The installation reflects on these cosmic ties between black holes, supernovas, and our own existence, inviting a deeper sense of wonder at the ties between our bodies and the genesis of galaxies.

It sits within a constellation of seven immersive works that shape 𝘉𝘳𝘦𝘢𝘵𝘩𝘦 | 𝘔𝘢𝘶𝘳𝘪 𝘖𝘳𝘢, where audiences journey from the roots of Amazonian trees to unseen inner worlds, moving between bodily rhythms and vast cosmic patterns — tracing breath as the rhythm that cultivates and connects all life.

📍 Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa @te_papa 
 𝘉𝘳𝘦𝘢𝘵𝘩𝘦 | 𝘔𝘢𝘶𝘳𝘪 𝘖𝘳𝘢: 𝘈𝘯 𝘐𝘮𝘮𝘦𝘳𝘴𝘪𝘷𝘦 𝘑𝘰𝘶𝘳𝘯𝘦𝘺 𝘪𝘯𝘵𝘰 𝘕𝘢𝘵𝘶𝘳𝘦
An @acmionline touring exhibition
Until 27 April 2026

Featured works:
𝘚𝘢𝘯𝘤𝘵𝘶𝘢𝘳𝘺 𝘰𝘧 𝘵𝘩𝘦 𝘜𝘯𝘴𝘦𝘦𝘯 𝘍𝘰𝘳𝘦𝘴𝘵 
𝘛𝘩𝘦 𝘑𝘰𝘶𝘳𝘯𝘦𝘺 𝘰𝘧 𝘉𝘳𝘦𝘢𝘵𝘩 (𝘌𝘷𝘰𝘭𝘷𝘦𝘳) 
𝘛𝘩𝘦 𝘉𝘳𝘦𝘢𝘵𝘩𝘪𝘯𝘨 𝘊𝘦𝘭𝘭 (𝘌𝘷𝘰𝘭𝘷𝘦𝘳) 
𝘛𝘪𝘥𝘦𝘴 𝘞𝘪𝘵𝘩𝘪𝘯 𝘜𝘴 (𝘗𝘳𝘪𝘯𝘵𝘴) 
𝘋𝘪𝘴𝘵𝘰𝘳𝘵𝘪𝘰𝘯𝘴 𝘪𝘯 𝘚𝘱𝘢𝘤𝘦𝘵𝘪𝘮𝘦
 𝘞𝘦 𝘓𝘪𝘷𝘦 𝘪𝘯 𝘢𝘯 𝘖𝘤𝘦𝘢𝘯 𝘰𝘧 𝘈𝘪𝘳 (𝘝𝘪𝘥𝘦𝘰 𝘦𝘥𝘪𝘵𝘪𝘰𝘯)
